concepts.title: Version Control with Git
concepts.body_text: Git records snapshots of your code over time so many people can change it safely and you can undo mistakes.

concepts.title: Code Review & Pull Requests
concepts.body_text: A pull request proposes changes so teammates can read, comment on and approve them before they join the main code.

concepts.title: Automated Testing
concepts.body_text: Tests are extra code that checks your real code still behaves correctly, run automatically on every change.
key_points: Green tests build confidence to change code

concepts.title: Clean Code & Refactoring
concepts.body_text: Refactoring improves the shape of code (names, structure) without changing what it does, keeping it easy to read and extend.
key_points: Small functions are easier to test

concepts.title: Arrays & Hash Maps
concepts.body_text: An array stores items in a numbered row; a hash map stores items by a key so you can fetch them almost instantly.
key_points: Choice depends on how you look data up

concepts.title: Big-O / Time Complexity
concepts.body_text: Big-O describes how the work a piece of code does grows as the input gets bigger, ignoring constant details.
key_points: O(1) constant, O(n) linear, O(n^2) quadratic

concepts.title: Tables, Keys & SQL Basics
concepts.body_text: Relational databases store data in tables of rows and columns; keys link tables and SQL is the language used to ask questions of the data.
key_points: Primary key uniquely identifies a row

concepts.title: Indexing
concepts.body_text: An index is a sorted lookup structure that lets the database find rows without scanning the whole table.
key_points: Speeds up reads, slows down writes

concepts.title: Transactions & ACID
concepts.body_text: A transaction groups several changes so they all succeed or all fail together; ACID names the guarantees that keep data correct.

concepts.title: Star Schema
concepts.body_text: A star schema models data as a central fact table of measurements surrounded by dimension tables that describe them, optimised for analytics.
key_points: Fact table holds measures and foreign keys

concepts.title: Slowly Changing Dimensions
concepts.body_text: SCD techniques decide how to handle a dimension attribute that changes over time (overwrite, keep history, or store the previous value).
key_points: Type 2 adds a new row per change

concepts.title: Normalization vs Denormalization
concepts.body_text: Normalization splits data to avoid duplication; denormalization deliberately duplicates it to make reads faster.

concepts.title: Batch vs Streaming
concepts.body_text: Batch processing handles a bounded chunk of data on a schedule; streaming processes never-ending data continuously with low latency.

concepts.title: Idempotency
concepts.body_text: An idempotent step produces the same result no matter how many times it runs, so retries and backfills are safe.

concepts.title: DAGs & Schedulers
concepts.body_text: A DAG (directed acyclic graph) describes tasks and their order; a scheduler like Airflow runs them on time and in the right sequence.
key_points: Nodes are tasks, edges are dependencies

concepts.title: Data Quality & Validation
concepts.body_text: Data quality checks verify incoming data is complete, valid and reasonable before it is trusted downstream.
key_points: Freshness matters as much as correctness
